school: 3rd Rightly-Guided Caliph — married two daughters of the Prophet; standardised the Quranic text (Uthmanic codex); expanded the Islamic empire to Afghanistan and North Africa
parents: 'Affan ibn Abi al-'As / Arwa bint Kuraiz bin Rabi'a
children: 'Abdullah bin 'Uthman , 'Abdullah bin 'Uthman(younger) , 'Aishah bint 'Uthman , Umm Aban bint 'Uthman , Umm 'Amr bint 'Uthman , 'Amr bin 'Uthman , Aban bin 'Uthman , 'Umar bin 'Uthman , Khalid bin 'Uthman , Maryam bint 'Uthman , al-Walid bin 'Uthman , Sa'id bin 'Uthman , Umm 'Uthman bint 'Uthman , Umm Khalid bint 'Uthman , Arwa bint 'Uthman , Umm Aban bint 'Uthman(younger) , Maryam bint 'Uthman (younger)
spouses: Ruqayyah bint Muhammad , Umm Kulthum bint Muhammad , Fakhitah bint Ghazwan , Ramlah bint Shayba bin Rabi'a , Umm 'Amr bint Jundub al-Dausi , Fatima bint al-Walid b. 'Abd Shams , Na'ilah bint al-Furafisah
quotes: If our hearts were truly pure, we would never have our fill of the words of God.
Do not be deceived by the good deeds of a man until you have observed what he does in moments of anger.

school: 2nd Rightly-Guided Caliph — transformed Islam into a world empire; conquered Persia, Egypt, Syria; established Islamic governance, welfare state and rule of law
parents: Khattab ibn Nufayl / Hantamah bint Hisham Makhzumi
children: ibn Umar , Hafsa bint Umar , 'Abdur Rahman bin 'Umar (Akbar) , 'Ubaidallah bin 'Umar , Zayd bin 'Umar , 'Asim bin 'Umar , 'Iyad bin 'Umar , Zaid (older) bin 'Umar , Ruqayyah bint 'Umar , Fatima bint 'Umar , 'Abdur Rahman(middle), 'Abdur Rahman(younger), Zainab bint 'Umar
spouses: Zaynab bint Maz'un , Umm Kulthum bint 'Amr (Jarwal) , Qaraybah bint Abi Umayyah al-Sugra , Jamila bint Thabit ibn Abi al-Aflah , 'Atikah bint Zayd , Umm Kulthum bint 'Ali bin Abi Talib , Umm Hakim , Umm Walad(Lihayyah), Umm Walad(Fakhihah)
quotes: Hold yourself to account before you are held to account — and weigh your deeds before they are weighed against you.
Learn Arabic, for it strengthens the mind and increases one's noble character.
The one who is most deserving of respect is the one who does not seek it.
Do not let your happiness depend on something you may lose.

school: 4th Rightly-Guided Caliph — cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet; considered the fountain of Islamic mysticism; his Nahj al-Balagha is the greatest treasury of Arabic wisdom
parents: Abu Talib ibn 'Abd al-Muttalib / Fatima bint Asad
children: Hassan ibn Ali bin Abi Talib , Hussain ibn 'Ali ibn Abi Talib , Zaynab bint 'Ali bin Abi Talib , Umm Kulthum bint 'Ali bin Abi Talib , Muhammad bin 'Ali bin al-Hanfiyyah , al-Abbas, 'Umar bin 'Ali bin Abi Talib , Ja'far, 'Uthman, Yahya (died young), Fatima bint 'Ali bin Abi Talib , others
spouses: Fatima bint Muhammad , Khawlah bint Ja'far , , Asma bint 'Umays , Umm Walad(s)
quotes: Do not be a slave to others when God has made you free.
Speak to people according to their level of understanding — do you want God and His Prophet to be denied?
The tongue is a beast — if you let it loose, it will devour you.
Two things define you: your patience when you have nothing, and your attitude when you have everything.

school: Abbasid Caliph — ruled at the peak of the Islamic Golden Age; contemporary of Charlemagne; the protagonist of A Thousand and One Nights; patron of science, poetry and music
quotes: The caliphate is not a robe you wear — it is a burden you carry.
Knowledge is the life of the mind, and the light of every era.
I look at a cloud and say: rain wherever you wish — your harvest will come to me regardless.
The caliph who does not weep for his subjects is not worthy of them.
